summary:
The proximal walls of a Class II cavity on a deciduous molar converge towards the occlusal surface due to the natural anatomic contour of the tooth. This design ensures adequate retention while preserving healthy dental tissue around the restoration site.

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{B}}$
